sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento del VIX del 11.31% a 26.78 e del calo dell'S&P 500 dell'1.51% a 6506.48. Il TLT è sceso dell'1.90% a 85.83, mentre il rendimento del decennale USA è salito al 4.39%. L'oro è salito dello 0.10% a $4574.90/oz. Il contesto geopolitico rimane teso a causa della guerra tra Israele e Iran.
sentiment.reason_en: The market sentiment is risk-off due to the 11.31% increase in VIX to 26.78 and the 1.51% decline in S&P 500 to 6506.48. TLT fell 1.90% to 85.83, while the US 10-year yield rose to 4.39%. Gold increased by 0.10% to $4574.90/oz. The geopolitical context remains tense due to the Israel-Iran war.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è stato colpito dalla guerra tra Israele e Iran, con l'S&P 500 che ha perso l'1.51%. Il VIX è aumentato significativamente, raggiungendo 26.78. Il mercato obbligazionario ha subito pressioni, con il TLT che è sceso dell'1.90%. L'oro ha guadagnato lo 0.10%. Il prezzo del petrolio è sceso del 5.15% a $106.41.
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market was hit by the Israel-Iran war, with the S&P 500 losing 1.51%. The VIX increased significantly to 26.78. The bond market came under pressure, with TLT falling 1.90%. Gold gained 0.10%. Oil prices dropped 5.15% to $106.41.

audio_script_it:
Mentre i mercati tradizionali osservano la consueta pausa del fine settimana, l'attenzione si concentra sugli asset digitali che continuano a operare 24 ore su 24. La seduta asiatica, che ha chiuso prima dell'inizio del weekend, ci consegna un Nikkei in calo del 3.38% a 53,373 punti e Shanghai sostanzialmente invariato a 3957.05 punti. Questi trend asiatici anticipano una possibile apertura cauta delle borse europee e americane nella prossima settimana.

Il sentiment attuale è decisamente risk-off, come evidenziato dai mercati occidentali nella chiusura precedente. L'S&P 500 ha perso l'1.51% a 6506.48 punti, mentre il VIX è salito dell'11.31% a 26.78, riflettendo un aumento dell'avversione al rischio. Il Dollar Index (DXY) ha mostrato una lieve flessione del 0.15% a 99.50, e il rendimento del decennale USA è salito al 4.39%.

Sul fronte geopolitico, le tensioni continuano a influenzare i mercati, con particolare attenzione alle dinamiche internazionali che potrebbero impattare sulle economie globali.

La macroeconomia si concentra sui prossimi dati in uscita, inclusi quelli sul PIL degli Usa e sull'inflazione dell'Eurozona, che saranno cruciali per le decisioni delle banche centrali.

Passando agli asset digitali, Bitcoin ha registrato una lieve crescita dello 0.24% a $70,695. Il Fear & Greed Index è a 12, in territorio di Extreme Fear. Ethereum è salito dello 0.19% a $2,155, mentre Solana e Binance Coin hanno mostrato variazioni positive.

Nella giornata odierna, con i mercati tradizionali chiusi, l'attenzione rimane focalizzata sulle crypto. I flussi negli ETF Bitcoin continuano a essere monitorati, con un inflow netto di $-52.0M.

Per la prossima settimana, sarà fondamentale monitorare l'evoluzione delle tensioni geopolitiche e i dati macroeconomici in uscita, che potrebbero influenzare significativamente i mercati sia tradizionali che digitali.

{'intro': "Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con un'analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.", 'deep_dive_bitcoin': 'Iniziamo con il Bitcoin, che attualmente quota $70,686, in calo dello 0.23% rispetto alla giornata precedente. Gli ETF Bitcoin hanno registrato un deflusso di $11.2 milioni (BTC ETF Daily Net Inflow). Tecnicamente, il prezzo del Bitcoin sembra essere in una fase di consolidamento, con un supporto chiave a $70,000.', 'altcoins': "Passando alle altcoin, Ethereum guadagna lo 0.19% e si posiziona a $2,155. Solana e Binance Coin (BNB) mostrano invece un andamento positivo, con guadagni rispettivamente dello 0.68% e dello 0%, quest'ultimo scambiato a $643.08. Tuttavia, BNB registra una lieve flessione dello 0.16%.", 'sentiment_fear_greed': "L'indice Fear & Greed è a 12, in territorio di Extreme Fear. Questo livello di paura estrema potrebbe indicare una possibile inversione del trend nel breve termine. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici, come l'inflazione e i tassi di interesse, resta alta. Gli ultimi dati macro USA, sebbene non recentissimi, mostrano un quadro di rallentamento economico, con un CPI YoY al 2.7% e un tasso di disoccupazione in salita al 4.4%.", 'conclusione': "In sintesi, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di attesa, con il Bitcoin che cerca di mantenere il supporto a $70,000 e le altcoin che mostrano un andamento misto. L'estrema paura degli investitori, insieme ai dati macroeconomici, potrebbe influenzare significativamente il trend futuro."}

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
{'introduction': "Welcome to your daily morning market briefing. Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.", 'sentiment': 'Global sentiment is risk-off today, with investors digesting recent economic data and geopolitical tensions. Key data points include the US GDP growth rate, which is yet to be released, and the recent PCE Core YoY figure of 3.1%.', 'traditional_markets': 'Traditional markets are closed for the weekend, but looking at the last available data: equities were under pressure, with the S&P 500 at 6506.48 (-1.51%) and the STOXX 600 at 56.70 (-1.77%). Bonds also saw significant movements, with the US 10Y Yield at 4.39% (+2.57%) and the TLT Bond USA 20Y at 85.83 (-1.90%). Currencies and commodities also experienced volatility, with EUR/USD at 1.15 (-0.02%) and Brent at $106.41 (-5.15%).', 'geopolitics_macro': "Geopolitical tensions continue to influence market sentiment, with ongoing concerns about the Iran crisis and its impact on global markets. The ECB's recent decision on interest rates and the Bank of England's stance on rates are also being closely watched. The macro outlook remains complex, with investors monitoring inflation and growth indicators closely.", 'transition_to_crypto': "While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which are open 24/7. Let's take a look at the current state of the crypto market.", 'crypto_market': 'The Fear & Greed Index is at 12, indicating extreme fear. Bitcoin is currently at $70,695 (+0.24%), with Ethereum at $2,155 (+0.19%), Solana at $90.02 (+0.68%), and BNB at $643.08 (-0.16%). The BTC ETF Daily Net Inflow is at $-52.0M, showing some improvement.'}

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a closer look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

The Fear & Greed Index is currently at 12, indicating extreme fear in the market. Bitcoin is trading at $70,686, down 0.23% from the previous day. From a technical analysis perspective, Bitcoin's price has been experiencing some fluctuations, but it's still holding above the $70,000 mark. The recent flows into Bitcoin ETFs have been mixed, with some days seeing significant inflows and others seeing outflows.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,155, up 0.19% from the previous day. Solana is at $90.02, up 0.68%, and Binance Coin is at $643.08, down 0.16%. Ethereum's price has been relatively stable, while Solana and Binance Coin have seen some minor gains and losses.

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index suggests that investors are currently extremely fearful. This could be due to the recent inflation concerns and the ongoing geopolitical tensions. The correlation between cryptocurrencies and traditional assets has been increasing, with many investors viewing Bitcoin as a risk asset. The current market conditions and the Fear & Greed Index indicate that investors are cautious, but it's essential to keep in mind that the cryptocurrency market is highly volatile and can change rapidly.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you in the next episode.

articles.title: Conflict and Consequences
articles.snippet: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranOn March 17, Israel announced — and Tehran later confirmed — the killing of Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council. If eliminating Ali Khamenei on the opening day of the U.S.-Israeli offensive struck down the regime’s supreme leader, Larijani’s death removes its supreme coordinator. A longtime fixture in the regime, Larijani appears to have been serving as the regime’s majordomo in recent months — and especially since Khamenei’s death on Feb. The post Conflict and Consequences appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: What the Donroe Doctrine Could Mean for China’s Economic Statecraft
articles.snippet: In the first few months of 2026, the Trump administration has embarked on military interventions in Iran and Venezuela and threatened possible annexation of Greenland. Meanwhile, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s February trip to Beijing followed on the heels of the first state visit to China by a British leader in eight years. As the United States and China prepare for their much-touted summit in late March, Beijing is weighing its options in the face of recent American actions and the new “Donroe Doctrine.”Chinese foreign policy remains driven by the core priorities of preserving regime security and consolidating influence in its The post What the Donroe Doctrine Could Mean for China’s Economic Statecraft appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Amid War, Yemen’s New Government Faces Challenges at Home and Abroad
articles.snippet: Yemen has a new government, formed after elements of the old one clashed on the battlefield. In this Q&A, Crisis Group expert Ahmed Nagi looks at its makeup and its prospects against the backdrop of Middle East war that may draw in the Houthi rebels.

articles.title: Why Did the United States Lift Sanctions on Assad’s Chemical Weapons Scientists?
articles.snippet: On June 30, 2025, as part of the process of normalizing relations with Damascus following the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, the Trump administration overhauled the sanctions imposed on Syria to facilitate the rebuilding of that war-ravaged country. In the course of revising these sanctions, however, 266 employees of the Scientific Studies and Research Center, the heart of the Assad regime’s chemical weapons complex, were removed without explanation from the U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Specially Designated Nationals List. This list is a compilation of human rights abusers, proliferators, terrorists, drug lords, and others deemed a threat to U.S. The post Why Did the United States Lift Sanctions on Assad’s Chemical Weapons Scientists? appeared first on War on the Rocks

articles.title: Where is the Israel-Hizbollah War Going?
articles.snippet: Israel and Hizbollah are once again fighting head to head, with disastrous repercussions for Lebanon. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Heiko Wimmen, David Wood and Max Rodenbeck consider what might come next.

articles.title: Why the Iran War May Have Just Killed the AI Boom
articles.snippet: The stock market spent the first week of the Iran war doing something strange: mostly shrugging. Oil spiked. Insurance markets effectively collapsed. Amazon had two data centers blown up. And the Nasdaq dipped, steadied, and the conversation shifted within days to whether the Fed might still cut in June. The prevailing read was: disruption, yes. Catastrophe, no. This thing will be over soon. I think that read is wrong. And wrong in a specific, structural way, not because the war will necessarily escalate further, but because the damage…

articles.title: U.S. Bets on Natural Gas to Power 10 GW AI Buildout in Ohio
articles.snippet: Amid the Iranian war and oil price crisis, the Department of Energy, alongside the Department of Commerce, has unveiled a public-private partnership with SoftBank and AEP Ohio that centers on one thing the AI boom desperately needs but rarely talks about plainly—power. Not theoretical power. Not intermittent power. Real, dispatchable, grid-ready megawatts, mostly from natural gas. At the core of the plan, revealed in a Friday press release, is a 10-gigawatt data center campus at the Portsmouth Site in southern Ohio, backed by an equally massive…

articles.title: Gold sees biggest weekly fall in 43 years as Iran war rages on
articles.snippet: Gold is also being impacted by rising anticipation that the US Federal Reserve won’t cut interest rates this year, while Fed chair Jerome Powell said inflation would rise.

articles.title: Why This Energy Shock Will Hit Consumers Harder Than 2011
articles.snippet: Arend Kapteyn, the global head of economics and strategy research and chief economist at UBS, told clients that one key reason the current Middle East conflict-driven energy shock "is not like 2011-2014" will be the absence of a comparable response from the shale patch, suggesting consumers are more likely to bear the brunt of the pain. Kapteyn noted that, on an inflation-adjusted basis, oil prices in 2011-2014 were actually higher than they are today, yet the U.S. economy absorbed that shock because the shale boom provided a lift to the…

articles.title: Oil Extends Rally as Hormuz Stays Closed
articles.snippet: Oil is heading for a fifth straight weekly gain, with Brent near $109, as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut for a third week, keeping supply tight despite policy efforts to ease the market. Friday, March 20, 2026 Oil markets are set to post their fifth consecutive weekly gain (excepting WTI which will see a minor week-over-week decline), with ICE Brent trading around $109 per barrel. Whilst the Trump administration has sought to defuse market concerns using any means available – waiving the Jones Act, easing Russian sanctions further or de-sanctioning…

articles.title: Two Russian Cargoes Offer Temporary Relief for Cuba's Energy Emergency
articles.snippet: Cuba is set to soon receive two Russian cargoes of crude and diesel amid the U.S. energy blockade that has caused an unprecedented power crisis in the country. The shipments from Russia, which considers Cuba as one of few “friendly countries”, could test the U.S. resolve to continue isolating energy shipments for the island. If the tankers en route to Cuba do arrive in the coming days, they would be the first Russian oil and fuel shipments to the country this year. The tanker Anatoly Kolodkin, sanctioned by the U.S.,…

articles.title: What happens to Bitcoin if oil price hits $180 per barrel?
articles.snippet: A 70% oil spike could nearly double US inflation, slash rate-cut hopes, and deepen downside risks for Bitcoin prices in the coming months.

articles.title: Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning Bitcoin price held near $70,000 as shifting Iran conflict risks and macro pressure weighed on markets, while derivatives and on-chain data signaled consolidation, not capitulation. This post Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.title: Bitcoin price aims to hold $70K amid rising inflation concerns
